Motion For Order Relieving Esponding Parties From Waiver
Off Calendar Jan-31-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff's motion for relief from waiver of objections as to request for production (set #1), form interrogatories (set #1), and special interrogatories (set #1) is denied. The motion does not establish that the failure to serve a timely response was the result of mistake, inadvertence, or excusable neglect. The Court makes no rulings as to any subsequent discovery but notes that the waiver provisions of CCP Sections 2030.290(a) and 2031.300(a) apply to a particular set of interrogatories or demands for inspection. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

Motion To Compel Responses To Inspection Demands
Off Calendar Nov-03-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Roberth J. Scott Jr. for defendant and moving party Leo Donian

NO OTHER APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Defendant's motion to compel responses to request for production of documents is ordered off calendar as it was not timely noticed. CCP Section 1005, as amended by AB 3078, Chapter 171, Statutes of 2004, requires that motions be served and filed 16 court days prior to the hearing, with five calendar days added if the motion was served by mail. The motion was filed and served on October 7, 2005. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
